# CONFUstudio — UI/UX Design Brief for Claude Design

## App Overview

CONFUstudio is a browser-based groovebox / music production workstation. Think Elektron Octatrack + VCV Rack + OP-1 — a hardware-inspired chassis UI with a modular patching canvas, all in vanilla HTML/CSS/JS (no framework, no build step).

**Tech stack**: vanilla JS (ES modules), CSS, Web Audio API, static file server.
**No framework, no bundler, no TypeScript.** All CSS is hand-written, all HTML is generated by JS at runtime.

## What to Redesign

The app currently works but looks dated and has UX friction. We need a coherent design system, clearer visual hierarchy, and modern interaction patterns.

### Key Pages (each rendered in `#page-content` div)

| #   | Tab     | Purpose                                                                                                  |
| --- | ------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | PATTERN | 16×64 step grid with track selector, per-step controls (probability, accent, param locks, micro-timing)  |
| 2   | PADS    | 4×4 velocity-sensitive pad grid for finger drumming / MIDI input                                         |
| 3   | ROLL    | Piano-roll MIDI editor with note bars, velocity lanes, zoom                                              |
| 4   | SOUND   | Per-track sound engine params (envelope, filter, LFO, engine type: plaits/clouds/rings/tone/sample/midi) |
| 5   | MIXER   | 8 channel strips + 2 bus + master with faders, pan, mute/solo, sends                                     |
| 6   | FX      | Global FX: delay (syncable), reverb (convolution), master drive, chorus                                  |
| 7   | MOD     | Modulation matrix: 8× modulation routes, 2 LFOs, 2 envelopes                                             |
| 8   | SCENES  | 8 scene slots for snapshot morphing between track param presets                                          |
| 9   | BANKS   | 8 banks × 16 patterns, copy/paste/swap patterns, naming                                                  |
| 10  | ARR     | Linear arrangement of scene sections, drag-to-reorder, loop regions                                      |
| 11  | SET     | Audio settings, MIDI config, theme picker, project import/export                                         |

### Top-level chrome (always visible)

From `index.html`:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [⏻] [▶] [■] [●] [Fill] [Tap]    SIG | VOL ▓▓▓▓▓  │
│         ┌─────────────────────────────────┐          │
│         │ CONFUstudio   A·01   122 BPM    │          │
│         │ [PAT][PADS][ROLL][SND][MIX][FX] │ 8×track  │
│         │ [MOD][SCN][BNK][ARR][SET]       │ channel  │
│         │                                 │ strip    │
│         │  ⟨PAGE CONTENT⟩                 │          │
│         │                                 │          │
│         │  ─── oscilloscope ───           │          │
│         └─────────────────────────────────┘          │
│  Keyboard / context panel                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### The Studio Canvas (modular patching)

A separate "studio" mode (zoomable/pannable canvas) with rack modules connected by SVG cables. Modules have ports, knobs, and internal params. This is the VCV Rack equivalent — users build custom signal chains visually.

## Deliverables

**Please deliver these files in a single response:**

### 1. `design-guide.md` — Design System Specification

- Color palette: primary, surface, text, accent, danger, success — with hex values
- Typography: font stack, sizes (px/rem), weights, line heights for UI / mono / display
- Spacing scale: 4px grid, padding/margin tokens
- Border radius tokens, shadow tokens
- Breakpoints: at least desktop (≥1024px) and tablet (≥768px)
- Dark theme variant (our current is dark-green, suggest improvements)
- Light theme variant (for users who prefer it)
- CSS custom property names that follow our existing `--chassis-*`, `--screen-*`, `--surface-*` pattern

### 2. `pattern-page.html` — Pattern Sequencer Redesign

The most important page. Current pain points:

- 8 tracks × 64 steps creates a huge scrollable grid
- Probability, accent, micro-timing controls are hard to discover
- Param locks editing is buried in sub-menus

Deliver as a standalone HTML file with embedded CSS. Include:

- Step grid with clear on/off/accent/probability states
- Track selector with mute/solo/sends visible without navigation
- Per-step detail panel (shows when a step is selected): probability, accent, velocity, micro-time, param locks, trig condition
- Pattern-level controls: length, swing, scale lock
- Keyboard shortcuts overlay (modal or persistent info strip)
- Make it responsive: collapses to single-track view on narrow screens

### 3. `mixer-page.html` — Mixer Redesign

Current pain points:

- 8 tracks squeezed into fixed space
- No visual feedback for levels (no meters)
- Bus routing is invisible

Deliver as standalone HTML with embedded CSS:

- 8 channel strips in horizontal scroll, each with: fader, pan knob, mute/solo buttons, signal meter (animated CSS), send knobs
- Master strip with larger fader and limiter indicator
- Bus 1 / Bus 2 strips
- Color-coded by track (use existing TRACK_COLORS array)
- Level meters animate with CSS custom properties (we'll drive from JS)

### 4. `component-library.html` — Reusable Component Gallery

Standalone HTML showing every UI component with states:

- **Button variants**: primary, secondary, ghost, danger, with active/hover/disabled states
- **Knobs**: circular rotary knob (0-100%), with SVG arc indicator, focus ring, label
- **Faders**: vertical fader with track, thumb, scale markings, value tooltip
- **Step**: off/on/accent/probability states, hover, selected, param-locked visual
- **Track strip**: full-width strip with number, color bar, name, mute/solo, mini-fader
- **Channel strip**: narrow vertical strip with meters
- **Dropdown / Select**: styled to match hardware aesthetic
- **Modal / Overlay**: for param locks editor, keyboard shortcuts, project settings
- **Toast notification**: positioning, slide animation, auto-dismiss
- **Tab bar**: active/inactive tabs with underline indicator
- **Chip / Badge**: for checkpoints, status indicators

Each component: HTML structure, CSS, and a brief note on JS interaction expectations.

### 5. `studio-canvas-redesign.md` — Studio Canvas UX Proposal

The current studio is a freeform zoom/pannable canvas with modules and cables. Proposal should address:

- Module layout: fixed grid snap vs free-form? Port positions (top/bottom/sides)?
- Cable routing: how cables visually route (bezier curves, color by signal type: audio=white, control=blue, event=yellow)
- Module interaction: drag to move, click to select, context menu on right-click, resize
- Port interaction: drag from port to create cable, snap to nearest compatible port
- Canvas controls: zoom buttons, fit-all, minimap?
- Signal graph visualization: overlay showing signal flow as graph viz (d3 or custom)
- Responsive: how does the canvas work on tablet/phone?

No code needed — just description, diagrams (ASCII or links to sketches), interaction patterns.

### 6. `integration-guide.md` — How These Map to Our Codebase

Brief explanation of how each deliverable maps to existing files:

| Deliverable    | Maps to                                                     | How to integrate                                   |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Pattern page   | `src/pages/pattern.js`, `src/css/drum-machine.css`          | Replace rendered HTML in `renderPage()`            |
| Mixer page     | `src/pages/mixer.js`, `src/css/mixer.css`                   | Replace `renderMixerPage()` HTML and CSS           |
| Components     | `index.html` chassis, `src/styles.css`                      | Adopt CSS custom properties, replace inline styles |
| Studio canvas  | `src/studio-modules.js`, `src/cables.js`, `src/modules.css` | Adopt port conventions, cable colors               |
| Step component | Reusable in pattern page and piano roll                     | New `src/components/step.js` if needed             |

## Constraints & Non-Negotiables

1. **No build step.** All CSS must be plain CSS (no SASS, LESS, PostCSS). All HTML must be inline or generated by JS `innerHTML`/`createElement`. No JSX, no templates.
2. **No framework.** No React, Vue, Svelte, etc. The app is vanilla JS.
3. **No runtime dependencies.** We can import CSS files and JS modules, but no npm packages that need bundling.
4. **CSS custom properties only.** Use `var(--token)` everywhere. No preprocessor variables.
5. **Touch-friendly.** All interactive elements need `min-height: 44px` touch targets.
6. **Accessibility.** Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for text, focus indicators, aria-labels on all controls, keyboard navigation for all interactive elements.
7. **Performance.** Pages render in <50ms, no layout thrashing, minimal DOM writes. Avoid animations that trigger layout (prefer transforms + opacity).
8. **Keep the chassis.** The hardware-inspired enclosure (army green bezel, dark screen area, channel strip on right) is a brand identifier. Improve it, don't remove it.
9. **Existing JS structure is stable.** We won't rewrite app.js or the page-switching mechanism. Assume `renderPage()` clears `#page-content` and calls the current page's render function. Your HTML/CSS must work within that pattern.
10. **Signal graph awareness.** Future pages will interact with `state.signalGraph` (audio routing graph). The studio canvas should anticipate node/connection visualization.

## Current CSS Tokens (for reference)

```css
:root {
  /* Chassis */
  --chassis-bg: #4e5f3c;
  --chassis-dark: #3d4d2e;
  --chassis-light: #5e7048;
  --chassis-text: #b8c8a0;
  --chassis-metal: #8a9a72;

  /* Screen */
  --screen-bg: #111810;
  --screen-text: #e8f4e0;
  --muted: #8aa880;
  --fg: #e8f4e0;
  --border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07);

  /* Accents */
  --accent: #f0c640;
  --live: #5add71;
  --record: #f05b52;
  --electric: #67d7ff;

  /* Surfaces */
  --bg: #0d150a;
  --bg2: #111c0e;
  --surface: #1a2615;
  --surface2: #243318;
  --text: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
  --text-dim: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.45);
  --text-muted: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25);

  /* Fonts */
  --font-mono: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
  --font-ui: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif;
}

/* Track colors */
--track-0: #f0c640; /* amber */
--track-1: #5add71; /* green */
--track-2: #67d7ff; /* sky */
--track-3: #ff8c52; /* orange */
--track-4: #c67dff; /* violet */
--track-5: #ff6eb4; /* pink */
--track-6: #40e0d0; /* teal */
--track-7: #f05b52; /* red */
```

## Success Criteria

I can take each deliverable and:

1. Copy the CSS custom properties into `src/styles.css`
2. Copy the component HTML patterns into each page's `renderPage()` function
3. Wire up the JS interactions following the notes in each deliverable
4. Have a coherent, good-looking UI without changing app.js or the page-switching mechanism
